What's in it for you?

· Lead high-impact data and analytics initiatives that drive strategic decision-making

Role Summary

We are seeking an experienced Project Manager - Data & Business Intelligence to lead the delivery of strategic data and reporting initiatives. The role is responsible for managing end-to-end project delivery, ensuring projects are delivered on scope, schedule, and within budget.

You will work closely with stakeholders across HR, International Admissions, and Digital Business teams to deliver modern reporting and analytics capabilities that provide actionable insights for decision-making. The role involves strong governance, stakeholder engagement, and management of internal teams and vendors in a complex technology environment.


Key Responsibilities

· Lead end-to-end delivery of business intelligence and reporting initiatives from project initiation through to completion.

· Define project objectives, scope, roles, and delivery approach while building and maintaining integrated project plans and schedules.

· Develop and manage governance artefacts including business cases, approval submissions, status reports, and risk/issue registers.

· Manage internal project resources and vendor engagements to ensure effective delivery outcomes.

· Oversee project budgets, cost tracking, and forecasting to ensure delivery within approved financial parameters.

· Coordinate project dependencies and provide regular updates to steering committees and governance groups.

· Engage and collaborate with stakeholders across business and technology teams to ensure alignment and successful project outcomes.

· Proactively manage risks and issues, implementing mitigation strategies to maintain project delivery timelines and quality.


Required Skills & Experience

Must Haves:


Extensive end‑to‑end project management experience delivering complex initiatives in a structured governance environment
Proven experience managing BI / reporting projects, not just general IT or application delivery or data migration
Demonstrated delivery of data‑led outcomes, including:
Data models/data sets
Executive and operational dashboards
Standardised and/or ad‑hoc reports
Strong hands‑on experience working with Power BI or equivalent BI platforms



Technical Capabilities & Experience

· Extensive end-to-end project management experience delivering complex initiatives within structured governance environments.

· Proven experience delivering Business Intelligence and reporting projects, particularly involving Power BI, data modelling, and BI reporting solutions.

· Demonstrated delivery of data-led outcomes, including datasets, executive dashboards, operational dashboards, and reporting frameworks.

· Strong financial management capability including budget management, cost tracking, and forecasting.

· Experience managing multiple projects concurrently, leveraging Agile or iterative delivery methodologies.

· Strong governance and documentation experience including preparation of project artefacts such as project initiation documents, RAID logs, and stage plans.

· Experience working in complex technology environments with multiple system integrations and dependencies.

· Strong stakeholder engagement skills with experience interacting with senior leadership and cross-functional teams.

· Excellent communication skills with the ability to present updates to governance boards and steering committees.

· Proficiency using Agile project management tools such as JIRA.

Qualifications & Clearance

· Relevant qualifications in Project Management, Information Technology, Business, or related discipline.

· PRINCE2, PMP, or equivalent project management certification highly desirable.

· Australian working rights required.

Lead learning that drives change and capability.

As Anglicare continues to deliver services that enrich lives and strengthen communities, we are seeking an experienced Training Lead to support the successful delivery and adoption of the Thread Program across our organisation.

As Training Lead, you'll play a key role in designing and delivering high‑quality learning solutions that enable staff capability, confidence and effective change adoption. You'll be welcomed into a compassionate, respectful and purpose‑driven organisation where we don't just talk about values - we live them.

About the Program

The Thread Program is a key initiative within Community and Mission (CaM) focused on improving client connection and service delivery. It brings together several projects and a new Salesforce CRM to simplify processes, strengthen collaboration and support a more seamless client experience across CaM services.

Your Opportunity

This is a full‑time, maximum‑term contract (12 months) within the Thread Program, working in close partnership with the Business Product Owner, Thread Program team and key stakeholders.

Working collaboratively with Subject Matter Experts, Change Managers and external implementation partners, you will oversee the design, development and delivery of learning solutions that support the successful rollout of program deliverables, including the Salesforce platform, operating model, processes and procedures.

About the Role

As the Training Lead, you will provide end‑to‑end leadership across learning needs analysis, design, development, delivery and evaluation. With strong learning expertise, attention to detail and a collaborative approach, you will:

Conduct training needs assessments to identify learning gaps and requirements aligned to program priorities

Work closely with the Business Product Owner, Thread Program team, Subject Matter Experts and vendors to define and validate learning requirements

Design and develop engaging eLearning, instructor‑led and blended learning solutions aligned to adult learning principles

Translate system functionality, processes and procedures into clear, practical and role‑relevant learning

Support the implementation and rollout of learning materials via the LMS and Connect intranet

Monitor learner engagement and evaluate the effectiveness of learning programs

Analyse feedback and learning data to drive continuous improvement

Prepare regular reporting on learning engagement, effectiveness and adoption

What You'll Bring

You are a confident, collaborative learning professional with strong instructional design expertise and experience supporting change initiatives. You will bring:

A relevant tertiary qualification in learning design, education or a related discipline

Minimum five years' experience in eLearning instructional design, ideally within aged care, healthcare or a regulated environment

Strong knowledge of adult learning principles and instructional design methodologies

Experience using digital authoring tools to create engaging learning content

Strong analytical, organisational and problem‑solving skills

Excellent written and verbal communication skills

A collaborative approach and ability to work effectively with diverse stakeholders

Why Anglicare?

We provide meaningful opportunities and choice - both for the individuals and communities we support, and for the people who make it happen.

At Anglicare, you'll enjoy the security, stability and opportunities that come with a strong, well‑established organisation that's ready for the future. You'll belong to a community of kind, humble and respectful people of all faiths and backgrounds.

In addition, you'll benefit from:

Flexible work options to support work-life balance

Salary packaging benefits - up to $15,900 tax‑free income plus $2,650 for meals and entertainment

Ongoing professional development and learning opportunities

Exclusive discounts with over 400 retailers

Employee Assistance Program and Fitness Passport with access to over 800 gyms

Are You Ready for a Career That Means More?

To apply, click Apply or submit your CV and a cover letter.
All applicants will receive a response.

Anglicare is as wonderfully diverse as the communities we support. While we are guided by Christian‑based values, our team includes people of all faiths and backgrounds who share our commitment to serving others.

About Us

We are a leading Australian energy retailer supplying electricity and gas to customers across major metropolitan markets. Our business operates at the intersection of wholesale energy markets, retail pricing, and renewable investment, with a strong focus on delivering commercially sustainable outcomes as the energy transition accelerates.

The Role

As an Energy Market Analyst, you will play a key role in commercial decision-making across wholesale electricity and gas portfolios. This role is focused on financial outcomes, supporting trading, origination, pricing, and investment decisions through applied market analysis. You will work closely with Trading, Wholesale Risk, Retail Pricing, and Investment teams to assess margin, exposure, contract structures, and renewable asset economics, translating market dynamics into clear commercial insights for senior stakeholders.

Key Responsibilities

Analyse wholesale electricity and gas market outcomes and translate them into financial and P&L impacts.

Support decisions across retail tariffs, hedging strategies, PPAs, and renewable investments.

Support trading and hedging activities across OTC contracts, ASX futures, PPAs, and environmental certificate markets.

Analyse price and volume risk and prudential exposure.

Support contract pricing, deal structuring, and origination opportunities.

Evaluate commercial viability of renewable and battery storage assets.

Academic & Professional Qualifications

Essential:

Degree or postgraduate qualification in Actuarial Studies, Finance, Commerce, Mathematics, or similar.

Proven track record in providing market insights that support commercial decisions.

Strong communicator with ability to translate complex market analysis into actionable insights.

Desirable:

3–6 years of relevant professional experience in energy markets analysis, modelling, or trading analytics with direct commercial exposure.

Strong understanding of wholesale electricity markets, ancillary service markets, and renewable/storage project economics.

Working knowledge of wholesale electricity market mechanisms including risk management and cost analysis across wholesale inputs is required.

Previous experience in energy trading, risk management, or financial markets.

Previous experience in project management of renewable energy assets.

Personal Qualities

Achievement orientated, humanistic, and affiliative.

Steady, calm, reliable, trustworthy, enthusiastic, and resilient.

Technical Skills

Proficiency in energy market simulation software.

Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills.

Strong organisational and project management capabilities.

Why Join Us?

Be part of a dynamic team shaping the future of energy in Australia.

Work on innovative renewable projects and cutting-edge market strategies.

Enjoy a collaborative environment with strong career growth pathways.

Ready to make an impact? Apply now and help us deliver smarter energy solutions for a sustainable future.

Salary: Base + super + corporate bonus plan + perks

Job Title: Machine Learning Engineer

APAC Region | Surry Hills, Sydney

Reports to: Machine Learning Lead




Company Overview: proprietary data platform processes billions of data points daily from cutting-edge IoT devices, empowering us to deliver insights on people flow, wayfinding, and queue management. From major international airport terminals to stadiums and smart cities, we enhance operational efficiency and elevate passenger experiences globally.




About the Role: Join a pioneering Australian-owned software company specialising in big data products that analyse human interactions. As a Machine Learning Engineer, you will collaborate closely with our engineering team on high-impact features, with a specific focus on our Computer Vision (CV) roadmap for airport environments. Your work will involve building robust models that track passenger journeys from "curb to gate," optimising terminal flow and enhancing security through automated spatial intelligence.




Responsibilities:

Drive innovation in machine learning models and algorithms to optimise our data insights platform and Intelligent Video Analytics capabilities.
Collaborate with big data engineers and product managers to implement high-throughput, real-time solutions for computer vision applications.
Conduct statistical analysis and apply ML techniques—including computer vision, spatial analytics, AI models, and time-series forecasting—to enhance product capabilities like queue management and passenger flow.
Design and develop robust codebases and pipelines using Python, C++, SQL, and Deep Learning frameworks, leveraging computer vision SDKs like NVIDIA DeepStream and GStreamer.
Utilise containerization technologies (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes) and cloud services for scalable, low-latency system design at the edge.
Foster an Agile environment with strong coding practices, code reviews, and continuous integration.




Requirements:

Experience: Minimum 4 years of experience as a Machine Learning Engineer or Data Scientist.
Core Languages: Strong proficiency in Python and SQL, and hands-on C++ with a focus on writing performance-critical code.
Computer Vision Stack: Proven experience with Computer Vision SDKs, preferably NVIDIA DeepStream and GStreamer for real-time video analytics.
Technical Expertise: Expertise in machine learning techniques, AI model development, statistical analysis, time-series forecasting, computer vision, and deploying real-time inference models.
Infrastructure: Experience with cloud technologies (preferably AWS) and containerisation (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes).
Soft Skills: Excellent communication skills and the ability to engage with stakeholders to understand complex business requirements in high-stakes environments.
Engineering Standards: Familiarity with version control (Git), Agile methodologies, and collaborative problem-solving.
Right to Work in Australia and residing in Sydney

Work on-site or remote work (with in-office collaboration days for employees with local offices). 
Annual Birthday leave.
Annual Learning & Development 
Continuous training and career development opportunities.
Inclusive workplace culture that values diversity, equity, and belonging.

Reporting to the Director, OceanOmics and Ecosystem Intelligence you will actively contribute to a team driving impact and outcomes for Natural Ecosystems by working productively and efficiently, collaborating closely with new and existing partners as well as internally within Minderoo's matrix structure. You will be a champion of our One Minderoo Culture, focusing where applicable on partners, stakeholders and beneficiaries to develop and execute a content strategy aligned with Minderoo's strategic goals.



Ecosystem Intelligence & OceanOmics aims to revolutionise how we measure, understand and conserve life in our oceans by realising the power of marine genomics and environmental DNA (eDNA). By interweaving new approaches and knowledge with existing methods, we strive to drive better decision-making and outcomes for our ocean ecosystems.



As a Senior Advisor in DNA-powered Fisheries, you will be responsible for identifying partners and navigating the fisheries landscape to accelerate the uptake of DNA-based tools and support outcomes across fisheries management, public awareness, marine protected areas, ecosystem- based management, and the interaction of fisheries with threatened biodiversity and habitats.



Working closely with other team members in Natural Ecosystems, you will provide domain expertise and strategic direction in interweaving DNA tools with other fisheries data (e.g. video, acoustic, catch and satellite data). The goal is to ensure the most appropriate tool is applied at the right time to support sustainable fisheries management.



This role, requiring 8+ years' post-graduate experience (PhD or equivalent on-job training), requires a Senior Advisor who is motivated to navigate, empower and collaborate with a diverse range of partners across sectors, rather than conduct research themselves. The position is ideally suited to someone with a proven track-record of collaborating with the fishing industry, fishers' communities, policymakers and scientists to accelerate change within the sector and drive outcomes.





Use expertise and strategy to identify world-leading partners that the Foundation can support to new approaches to fisheries science and ecosystem-based management. Identify and assess non-traditional and emerging data and technology partners that may deliver step-change advances in ecosystem intelligence.
Support partners in drafting funding agreements, setting milestones, framing budgets. Where appropriate, represent Minderoo on scientific or technical advisory boards to monitor progress, guide delivery and inform strategic decisions. Work in close collaboration with Minderoo's Legal, Finance and Impact & Insights teams.
Complement existing expertise in fisheries science with a new suite of rapidly emerging DNA-tools in areas such as fish life history, relative abundance, genetic diversity and DNA-based metrics that infer ecological integrity. Understand and help address barriers to the adoption of DNA tools and develop strategies to facilitate their uptake.
Leverage data and technology, including models, metrics, AI tools and visualisation of fisheries and biodiversity data that can inform decision-making. Awareness that no information is useful if people can't use it. This position will have a focus on the 'eDNA plus' and 'eDNA data impact & policy' projects within the Ecosystem Intelligence body of work.
Effectively communicate the purpose of DNA-informed fisheries by working closely with your Natural Ecosystems colleagues, the Advocacy & Engagement team and our partners to produce media content, outreach materials, scientific reports/publications, and presentations for national and international events.
Working across national boundaries will require meetings outside normal working hours and likely involve international travel.

Benefits

Attractive parental leave

Job Title: Lead Financial Analyst (Finance SME) People Systems Consolidation
Ad Text:
Company description:



About Brisbane Catholic Education
Brisbane Catholic Education (BCE) is a vibrant learning community within the Archdiocese, employing over 12,000 people across a diverse range of positions. As the largest provider of Catholic education in Queensland, BCE is dedicated to delivering high-quality education from Prep to Year 12. Through strong partnerships with parents, governments, and the wider community, BCE fosters social responsibility, self-discipline and Catholic values in all its students. We offer a supportive working culture where care for the individual is an important part of our ethos. Arrive at work every day to a welcoming and compassionate environment, knowing that you'll be supported to take care of what's important to you. Learn more about Brisbane Catholic Education.



Job description:



Summary

The Lead Financial Analyst (Finance SME) plays a critical role in delivering a major People Systems Consolidation program. In this senior position, you will represent Financial Services across payroll, time, reimbursements and learning systems, ensuring finance outcomes are accurate, auditable and sustainable. Working closely with Finance, People & Culture and Technology teams, you will shape integration design, governance, controls and reporting to strengthen financial integrity and support enterprise‑wide transformation. Your expertise will directly influence how finance outcomes are embedded into future‑state people systems.

Location: BCEO Woolloongabba
Work hours: Fulltime | Flexible Working Arrangements Available
Status: Fixed Term | April 2026 - July 2027 (Possibility for Extension)
Salary: $155,351 - $172,606 plus super
Ad Close date: 16 March 2026
Contact: CareersBCE@bne.catholic.edu.au
About the opportunity

In this position you will:

Act as the Finance SME for the People Systems Consolidation Program, representing Financial Services in design, governance and decision‑making.
Define end‑to‑end finance processes, controls and future‑state designs across payroll and people‑related activities.
Lead finance integration requirements into the enterprise finance platform (D365), ensuring accurate payroll and expense accounting.
Establish finance governance, controls, audit trails and reconciliation processes to support compliance and reporting integrity.
Define finance reporting and analytics requirements for schools and corporate stakeholders.
Partner with payroll, people systems and technology teams to ensure data integrity across integrated systems.
Support finance requirements traceability, testing and validation including SIT, UAT and parallel pay activities.
Support cutover, go‑live and transition to BAU, providing finance input into operating procedures and hypercare.
About you

To be successful in this position you will bring relevant contemporary skills and experience including:  

Senior experience in finance systems and financial accounting within large, complex organisations, with strong focus on data integrity, timeliness and auditability.
Deep understanding of payroll‑to‑general‑ledger accounting, including costing allocations, provisions and reconciliations, translating payroll operations into accurate accounting outcomes.
Proven experience defining and assuring integrations between people systems and enterprise finance platforms, covering data mapping and transformation logic
Strong capability designing governance and control frameworks for integrated systems, including audit trails and evidence standards
Solid working knowledge of enterprise reporting and analytics, including defining requirements for dashboards and reporting platforms impacted by payroll and learning data.
Proven experience in process documentation and redesign, including SME facilitation, capture of business rules, and development of clear process maps and work instructions.
Strong delivery, stakeholder engagement and communication capability across requirements, design, testing and go‑live phases, with a track record producing high‑quality artefacts and driving adoption of improved ways of working.
Degree qualification in accounting, finance, commerce or a related field and hold a CA/CPA

Eligibility and Safeguarding requirements:

Eligibility to work in Australia for the duration of the appointment
Ability to obtain a National Criminal History check prior to commencement.
Why choose BCE

Join us and be part of the largest Catholic Diocese in Queensland, nurturing your personal and professional development goals. As a valued member of our organisation, enjoy these and other rewards:

Opportunities for career advancement and professional development
Competitive remuneration and flexible work arrangements (position dependent)
Salary packaging and salary-sacrificing options
Active staff social club and wellness programs
Personal support for our employees and families through the Employee Assistance Program.
Fitness Passport access 

Please note applications for this position may be considered as they are received, and an appointment made prior to the advertised close date.

Everyone at BCE models and fosters a commitment to the safeguarding of children and promotes a culture where student protection is everyone's responsibility. Our commitment to these standards requires that we conduct working with children checks and background referencing for all persons who will engage in direct and regular involvement with children and young people and/or adults-at-risk. Senior Data Engineer

Sydney | $180k + Super

If you are the type of engineer who enjoys understanding how data moves through systems - pipelines, warehouses, storage layers and infrastructure - this role may interest you.

You would be joining a small B2B software company where data sits close to the core of the product. The team is technical, curious and hands-on. There is no management layer here - you will work directly with the CTO and other engineers to shape how data flows through the organisation.

What You Would Be Doing

Your focus will be building and evolving the company's data platform.

That includes:

Designing and improving data pipelines

Working with AWS data infrastructure

Managing and optimising the Redshift warehouse

Structuring and modelling messy source data

Ensuring data moves reliably across systems

Supporting the reporting and analytics layer

This role suits someone who enjoys building systems and solving complex data problems rather than managing people.

The Environment

You would be working with a stack that currently includes:

Python

SQL

AWS

RDS (Postgres)

Redshift

AWS Glue

Terraform

Some legacy MySQL

QuickSight today, with interest in Power BI

The engineering team is also experimenting with AI-assisted development and internal tooling that allows safe exploration of replicated data environments.

What Matters Most

Tools are useful, but mindset matters more.

You will likely enjoy this role if you:

Like understanding how data flows through systems

Enjoy fixing and improving data pipelines

Take pride in building reliable infrastructure

Prefer hands-on engineering rather than management

Are naturally curious about how systems can be improved

The Team

You would be joining a small, highly capable engineering team that values curiosity, technical depth and thoughtful problem solving.

This role tends to suit engineers who enjoy tackling complex technical problems but prefer working in a smaller environment where they can have meaningful ownership of the systems they build.

Sydney based - Hybrid work environment.

Australian working rights essential.

Play a pivotal role delivering strong outcomes to our members through shaping investment risk decisions across public and private markets at Australian Retirement Trust. This role offers autonomy, influence and the opportunity to partner with investment leaders in one of Australia’s largest super funds.

Why join us?

You'll get 14% super as standard, to help your future come alive.
Unleash your potential and build a career to be proud of through excellent training and development opportunities.
Balance your work between in-person days at our purpose-built hubs and working from home.
In the office, enjoy end of trip facilities, a yoga and multi-faith room, parent and carer rooms, quiet rooms, BrewHub coffee facilities, and collaborative team spaces.

About the role

Join Australian Retirement Trust as a Senior Risk Specialist, Investment Risk, a technical role within the Risk & Compliance function operating as part of the second line of defence. Reporting to the Senior Manager, Investment Risk, you’ll provide specialist risk advice, analysis and insight across public and private market investments.

You’ll work closely across the investment ecosystem, partnering with Investment, Investment Operations, Compliance and Risk specialists to support effective risk management, governance and decision-making.

This role offers a high level of autonomy and professional judgement, positioning you as a trusted risk partner who delivers practical, proportionate challenge to support strong member outcomes. You’ll also contribute to the ongoing maturity of ART’s investment risk frameworks, reporting and practices.




Key aspects of the role include:

Provide specialist investment risk advice, analysis and insights across public and private markets

Assess investment strategies, products, initiatives and projects with a risk lens

Partner with Investment and Investment Operations teams to support sound, risk-informed decision-making

Support investment risk management processes, including risk and control assessments and issue management

Identify, assess and escalate emerging risks and control weaknesses in collaboration with first-line stakeholders

Monitor investment risk exposures and key risk indicators, escalating concerns where required

Contribute to high-quality investment risk reporting for senior management and governance forums

Support the application and continuous improvement of investment risk frameworks, policies and standards

Assist with regulatory reviews, audits and assurance activities relevant to investment risk

About you

It goes without saying you'll be a great communicator with top notch interpersonal skills. We'll also expect you to pick up problems and come up with quick, creative ways to solve them. It's quite likely you tick some of the following boxes too:  

Demonstrated experience in investment risk management within investment management, financial markets, trading, private assets and investment operations environments

Strong understanding of investment risks across public and private markets, including complex and illiquid asset classes

Proven ability to provide practical, commercially balanced risk advice that supports sound investment decision-making

WorkSafe is Victoria's workplace health and safety regulator. We are also the workplace injury insurer. Our job is to reduce workplace harm and improve outcomes for injured workers. This is our promise to the Victorian community and the reason we exist.





Integration Solution Designer

$152,556.00 per annum + 12% superannuation
Full time, ongoing opportunity
Wadawurrung/Geelong based role (hybrid work from home - 3 days a week in office)

About the opportunity

Business Unit - Strategic Delivery, Technology Operations

At WorkSafe Victoria, our purpose is clear: to reduce workplace harm and improve outcomes for injured workers. This is our promise to the Victorian community. Our Technology Operations team plays a vital role in delivering robust data and integration platforms that power insights, analytics, and decision-making to support safer workplaces and better recovery for injured workers.

This is an exciting opportunity for a skilled Integration Solution Designer to shape and deliver high-impact data and integration solutions. You will create detailed design artefacts, lead technical specifications for cloud-based data platforms, and provide expert guidance to ensure solutions are secure, scalable, and aligned with enterprise architecture. Working in a dynamic environment, you will drive innovation in integration frameworks while contributing to our goal of meaningful change for Victorians.

Key accountabilities:

You will be designing and leading the detailed technical design of complex integration and data solutions.
You will lead troubleshooting, specification, and implementation to ensure compatibility and supportability.
You will create high-quality design documentation and provide specialist advice that balances functional, security, and performance needs.

Please ensure you review the position description here for additional information on accountabilities for this role.

About you

You are an experienced integration and data solutions professional who wants to make a difference in workplace safety and community outcomes. Your communication and organisational skills are excellent. You have a passion for translating complex technical requirements into practical, high-quality designs that deliver real value.

You bring proven expertise in designing and delivering data/integration solutions, with a strong focus on frontend analytics tools and robust querying capabilities. You thrive in independent and collaborative settings, applying expert knowledge to end-to-end problem resolution.

Mandatory skills and experience/must have:

Strong expertise in SAS and WebFOCUS for data analytics, reporting, and visualisation.
Advanced SQL skills for complex querying, data transformation, and performance optimisation across large datasets.
Demonstrated ITIL knowledge (v3 or v4 certification highly desirable) to support service management, incident resolution, and delivery frameworks.
Experience designing solutions using cloud data engineering tools (e.g., SAS EM, AWS Glue/Lambda, Azure Data Factory/Synapse) and ETL platforms (e.g., SAS Data Integration Studio, Informatica or Talend).
Proven ability to produce detailed design artefacts, conduct impact analysis, and lead technical reviews for integration and data components.

Working with us

At WorkSafe, our goal is for every employee to feel connected, safe, and valued within their team, for teams to feel appreciated in the workplace, and for WorkSafe to be respected in the community.

We believe that a diverse workforce is essential for delivering the best outcomes for the Victorian community. We strongly encourage applications from all individuals, including First Nations people, people with disabilities, those from diverse cultural and linguistic backgrounds, and individuals of all sexualities and genders.

What's next?

Applications close 11:00pm Thursday 26th March 2026. Click the link to apply, and join us in making a difference to Victorian lives.





Only applications submitted via the WorkSafe Careers website will be considered.

Company description:



For over 45 years, Rawson Group has helped thousands of Australians make their dream home a reality, taking pride in our beautiful designs and quality build.

From humble beginnings in country NSW, we have grown to be one of the largest and most trusted builders and land developers in NSW and the ACT.



Job description:



About the role:

Rawson Group are searching for a senior leader to join us as the Head of Estimating. Where you will lead the strategic direction, governance and performance of the estimating function across Rawson Group. This is a senior leadership opportunity to shape the future of estimating at Rawson Group and influence outcomes at both operational and strategic levels. Reporting to the General Manager, Operations, this role is responsible for building a best‑in‑class capability that strengthens commercial outcomes, supports margin discipline, improves operational efficiency and enables scalable growth. You'll provide leadership across our Sales, Production, Masters Estimating & Cost Planning divisions, ensuring consistent methodology, accurate data, aligned systems and processes across the business.

A key focus of the role is establishing a high‑performing estimating operating model that embeds continuous improvement and lifts the maturity of the function. You'll drive collaboration across internal teams and external partners, ensuring the estimating function is tightly integrated with broader business objectives and project delivery requirements.

Does this sound like the next step in your career? If so, Apply now!

Why Join Us?

More than a place to work, Rawson Group is a place to grow. Join our team and you'll be rewarded with:

Hybrid work balance of Wednesday & Friday WFH
Optional 5 weeks paid leave
Friendly, social and collaborative team environment
Company Wide Reward and Recognition Platform, where points are redeemable for cash vouchers or days off
Recruitment Referral Program - up to $5000
Discount on home builds and land packages
Access to our employee assistance program for you and your family
Paid parental leave
Gym membership discounts


Rawson Group is proud to be ranked as the 5th largest detached home builder in N.S.W. & A.C.T and we're still growing! We believe our people are key to success. We foster a culture of appreciation, support, and internal growth. We promote from within, invest in training and development, and offer flexible work-from-home options to help you thrive both professionally and personally. Apply today and be part of something great!

Key Responsibilities:

Define and execute the Group's estimating strategy aligned to Rawson's commercial, operational, and growth objectives.
Establish a long-term vision for the estimating function, transitioning from transactional estimating to a strategic commercial capability.
Develop and implement a best-practice estimating framework across sales, production, and cost planning.
Embed an Ideal Operating Model (IOM) for estimating, including structure, capability, capacity, systems, and governance.
Lead and integrate the sales estimating, production estimating, cost planning, and masters & systems teams to operate as a cohesive, high-performing function.
Build leadership capability & oversee workload planning within the estimating leadership team through coaching, mentoring, and performance management.
Establish and maintain margin governance frameworks across sales and production estimating.
Provide oversight of estimating assumptions, contingencies, risk allowances, and commercial levers.
Build strong relationships with external consultants and offshore partners to enhance capability and performance.
Develop and implement a comprehensive reporting framework for estimating performance, productivity, accuracy, and margin outcomes.

About You:

Qualifications:

Tertiary:

Bachelor's degree in construction management, Quantity Surveying, Engineering, Commerce, or a related discipline (preferred).
Postgraduate qualification in Business, Project Management, Construction Economics, or MBA (not-essential).

Professional:

Experience in a similar role is essential.
RICS (Royal Institute of Chartered Surveyors) is desirable.
AIQS (Certified Quantity Surveyor or equivalent) is desirable.

Skills:

Proven and strong experience with liaising with senior stakeholders of an organisation.
Ability to analyse and resolve complex problems which may require the involvement of multiple parties.
Senior leadership experience in estimating, cost planning, or related disciplines.
Proven ability to design and implement high-level operating models and process frameworks.
Strong commercial acumen with demonstrated success in margin management and cost governance.
Experience leading multi-disciplinary teams and driving organisational change.
Advanced stakeholder management and executive communication capability.
Experience working with offshore or external delivery models.

Attributes:

High level communication, influencing and negotiation skills in stakeholder management to achieve positive outcomes.
Ability to work under pressure, resilience and "drive" to succeed when faced with obstacles.
Strong analytical and strategic thinking skills with the ability to translate data into commercial insight Work as part of a broader team.

Overview


Cloud Operations + Innovation (CO+I) is the team behind one of the world’s largest Cloud Infrastructures, responsible for powering all Microsoft online Products and Services as well as powering Microsoft’s “Cloud First” mission. Our focus is on smart growth, high efficiency, and delivering a trusted experience to customers and partners worldwide, and we are looking for passionate, high energy program managers to help achieve that mission.

If you want to have fundamental impact on how Microsoft’s Cloud is deployed and operated globally, want to be involved with one of the most strategically important teams at Microsoft, if strong technical and business acumen, leadership, creativity, and out of the box thinking are some of the skills you bring to the table - this job is for you!

We are looking for experienced technical program managers with deep technical and business expertise, extraordinary leadership and demonstrated large-scale technical program management success. You should have exceptional industry knowledge, technical depth in the execution and delivery of datacenters, and have the executive presence to lead complex and time sensitive initiatives.In alignment with our Microsoft values, we are committed to cultivating an inclusive work environment for all employees to positively impact our culture every day.

Responsibilities

Lead Strategic Discovery & Intake - Drive structured discovery for complex or ambiguous business needs, articulating the business problem, aligning stakeholders, and translating requirements into actionable solution roadmaps.

Develop Innovative, Actionable Solutions - Design creative, scalable solutions that ensure measurable outcomes such as improved delivery predictability, schedule efficiency, and improved business outcomes.

Communicate with Impact - Deliver clear, concise, and compelling communication across executive stakeholders and project teams.Use storytelling and visual dashboards to foster alignment and transparency.

Lead Cross-Functional, Global Teams - Manage geographically dispersed teams, building strong connections across engineering, operations, finance, and business units.Drive collaboration, resolve conflicts, and champion change management for successful adoption.

Drive Business Results Through Data - Establish and monitor KPIs and OKRs tied to business goals.Apply data-driven insights to optimize timelines, quality, and cost performance.

Apply Technical & Market Expertise - Maintain fluency in technical architecture, integration points, and competitive ecosystem trends.Use this knowledge to anticipate challenges and deliver differentiated solutions.

Champion Process Excellence & Automation - Continuously improve delivery processes and tools, leveraging automation and AI-driven analytics.Implement predictive insights and workflow automation to enhance scalability and efficiency.

Enable Intelligent Reporting & Governance - Design and implement self-service BI dashboards and reporting frameworks.Ensure data accuracy, compliance, and security across all solution delivery metrics.

Manage Ambiguity & Risk - Navigate projects with unclear requirements and incomplete data through proactive risk management and scenario planning.Maintain resilience and adaptability in dynamic business environments

Qualifications

Bachelor’s Degree and 4+ years of technical project / program management experience, or equivalent experience.

2+ years experience managing cross-functional and/or cross-team projects.

Full lifecycle technical program management methodology (PMP, Prince 2, etc) experience planning and managing scope, schedule, budget, quality, people, risk and communications

Experience in organizational change management and business process improvement

Should have a mix of technical and program management skills and experience

Strong interpersonal, communication and presentation skills

While not required, we also look for the following  Preferred Qualifications:

Demonstrates deep subject matter expertise on key functional areas of mechanical, electrical, networking, construction, or building automation systems

8+ years of program management / datacenter construction / network engineering experience; self-motivated, confident working at a senior executive level and comfortable working in a matrixed organization

6+ years experience managing cross-functional and/or cross-team projects.

Professional PM certification, PMP, ESi, PMI, Prince 2 or equivalent

Demonstrably clear leadership skills and experience working with large, diverse teamsUnderstanding of business and financial objectives and plans accordingly

Exceptional executive level communication and engagement skillsAbility to work in a flexible, fast-paced environment

Track record of coaching teams and mentoring others in planning, organizing and coordinating their work.

The ideal candidate possesses the following:

MBA or equivalent advanced degree

Experience applying Earned Value Management techniques to measure project performance

Experience managing large scale construction or network infrastructure projects

Exceptional problem identification and problem resolution skills

Understanding of contract language

Base salary $150,000 – $210,000 + 12% super + discretionary bonus + private healthcare + benefits package. Sydney-based, hybrid.

Prepare high-quality risk reporting and insights for senior leaders and governance forums

Advise on emerging risks, including escalation of issues and breaches

Lead scenario analysis, stress testing and forward-looking risk assessments

Support regulatory reviews, audits and assurance activities related to investment risk

Lead, coaching and developing a high-performing investment risk team

We currently have two positions available:

· Permanent and

· 12-month fixed term.

About you

It goes without saying you'll be a great communicator with top notch interpersonal skills. We'll also expect you to pick up problems and come up with quick, creative ways to solve them. It's quite likely you tick some of the following boxes too:  

Extensive experience in investment risk management within investment management, financial markets or complex institutional environments

Strong understanding of risks across public and private markets, including trading activities, private assets, and investment operations

Proven ability to provide independent, commercially focused risk advice and challenge to senior investment stakeholders

Demonstrated experience assessing investment strategies, structures and products from a risk perspective

Deep knowledge of regulatory and prudential requirements relevant to superannuation, investment risk and financial markets

Highly developed analytical skills, with experience in scenario analysis, stress testing or forward-looking risk assessments

Confident communicator with the ability to influence, engage and present clearly at senior leadership and governance forums

Established people leader with a track record of building high-performing, capable and engaged teams

Professional qualifications in risk, finance or investments (e.g. CFA, FRM, CPA or equivalent) are well regarded

About us

Our 2.4 million members trust us to take care of over $330 billion in retirement savings. And we help them take charge of their finances and face the future with confidence. Our inclusive culture means you’ll be valued and heard in a respectful workplace. We've created an environment where you can be yourself and do amazing work. It's that simple.

Apply online now

Everyone is welcome to apply. We value diverse thinking, cultures, perspectives, backgrounds, and abilities. Awaken your career at Australian Retirement Trust and apply now. Applications close 26th March 2026

We don’t want to leave you with a monstrous wait. So, you’ll hear from us after the closing date to find out whether you’ve made it through to the next round or not. Being fair and up-front is important to us – and recruitment is no different.

At AustralianSuper, we truly care about our colleagues. We know work and life are intertwined. That's why we support the diverse needs of everyone and have policies that enable us all to thrive and be truly flexible. We ensure diversity is celebrated for the opportunity it provides us all to learn and grow and deliver better outcomes for members. We are dedicated to building a high‑performance environment underpinned by a strong risk culture, ensuring we deliver the best outcomes for our members.


Your New Role

We're looking for a Senior Analyst - Risk Enablement to join our Group Risk team on an 18-month fixed term contract, based in Melbourne with hybrid working.

Reporting to the Senior Manager, Risk Strategy & Enablement, this role offers variety and exposure across multiple risk domains. You'll provide second line support to subject matter experts and leaders, contributing to both business-as-usual activities and strategic initiatives. Your work will span a variety of activities to support Group Risk colleagues implementing a risk transformation project either through their project or key business as usual deliverables.

Key duties include but are not limited to:

Support Group Risk SMEs and Leads with BAU tasks across five risk departments.
Support implementation and embedment of risk transformation projects.
Assist with controls testing, risk assessments, and policy updates.
Perform data analysis and reporting to inform decision-making.
Maintain GRC records and coordinate workshops, training, and policy reviews.
Develop training materials and collateral for risk initiatives.
Manage the Group Risk Enablement workplan and associated reporting.
Coordinate monthly communications such as the 'In the Know' Bulletin and 3 Lines Forum.
Promote compliance and risk awareness across the organisation.


What You'll Need

3+ years' experience in risk management within financial services or consulting.
Strong understanding of risk and control practices, frameworks, and assurance activities.
Experience in risk assessments, control reviews, and risk reporting.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office, especially Excel and PowerPoint.
Critical thinking and analytical skills with a continuous improvement mindset.
Excellent communication skills and ability to build collaborative relationships.
Tertiary qualifications in Business, Commerce, Law or a related field.
Experience with RSA Archer GRC tool and/or PowerBI.
Exposure to risk in change programs.
Accreditation or willingness to obtain accreditation in Risk Management (e.g., RMIA or GIA).
Ability to translate regulatory requirements (e.g., APRA) into practical business outcomes.


Life at AustralianSuper

AustralianSuper is committed to colleague development, and we support our people with ongoing learning, coaching and training, as well as career opportunities across our expanding global organisation. We offer generous leave entitlements and promote a blended working environment in which all roles can flex, and we're happy to discuss what this looks like for you.

We cultivate a workplace that champions safety, respect, inclusiveness and diversity. We are committed to supporting our diverse workforce in a way that is inclusive and embraces diversity in all its forms. If you require any reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process or the role, please let our recruitment team know.

What's Next

Apply now, if you share our values of Energy, Integrity, Generosity of Spirit and Excellent Outcomes and would like the opportunity to work in a challenging, growing and rapidly evolving team to deliver outstanding results.

AustralianSuper uses AI to review resumes and conduct initial phone interviews. This involves processing your resume, cover letter, and voice recording in Australia and the USA to help assess your suitability for the applied role. When invited to an AI phone screening, you may opt for a traditional phone screening instead. For information on how we handle your personal data, please refer to our Candidate Privacy Notice: https://www.australiansuper.com/careers/candidate-privacy-notice

Agencies please note: this vacancy is being managed directly by AustralianSuper's Talent Acquisition team. We will contact our preferred agency partners should we require additional support. Thank you.

Job Title: Customer Insights and Market Intelligence Manager
Ad Text:
Customer Insights & Market Intelligence Manager
Melbourne South-East | Permanent

A well-established national B2B organisation operating across industrial and infrastructure sectors is seeking a Customer Insights & Market Intelligence Manager to join its Customer Experience function.  The business provides essential equipment, services and engineered solutions to critical industries including mining, defence, utilities, marine and major infrastructure. With operations across Australia and a strong customer base, the organisation is now investing in stronger customer insight capabilities to support a shift toward a more customer-centric and solutions-focused operating model.  This high profile role will sit within the Customer Experience team and play a key part in unlocking the value of the organisation’s extensive customer and operational data.

The Opportunity
Reporting to the Head of Customer Experience, this role will lead the development of the organisation’s customer insights capability. You will work across multiple internal data sources to identify patterns, uncover commercial opportunities and translate insight into clear strategic actions for the business.

The organisation holds a significant volume of customer information across systems such as Salesforce, enterprise data warehouses and operational platforms. The focus of this role is to bring structure to that data environment, generate meaningful insight and ensure those insights influence decision making across sales, marketing and operational teams.

Key areas of responsibility include:

Analysing customer and operational data to identify growth opportunities within the existing customer base
Developing segmentation models and prioritising high-value customer accounts
Supporting the organisation’s customer data roadmap in partnership with IT and external analytics partners
Delivering executive-level reporting and insight dashboards that track customer performance and engagement
Leading Voice of Customer initiatives including NPS analysis and structured customer research programs
Supporting the development of digital customer engagement platforms designed to strengthen relationships with key accounts
Partnering with sales teams to identify cross-sell and upsell opportunities across complex customer environments
Working cross-functionally with sales, marketing, IT and operations to embed customer insights into strategic decisions
The role sits at the intersection of data, strategy and customer experience, requiring both analytical depth and strong stakeholder engagement.  There is also a clear leadership pathway attached to the position, with the opportunity to grow into a broader Customer Experience leadership role over time.

About You
You bring experience in customer insights, market intelligence, commercial analytics or CX strategy, ideally within complex B2B or industrial environments.  You are comfortable working with large datasets and translating analysis into practical commercial insight. Just as importantly, you can communicate those insights clearly to senior stakeholders and influence decision making across the organisation.
You will likely bring:

Strong analytical capability and experience working with CRM or enterprise data platforms
Experience developing customer segmentation, market insight or Voice of Customer programs
The ability to translate complex data into clear strategic recommendations
Confidence presenting insights and reports to senior leadership teams
A collaborative mindset and the ability to work across multiple business functions
Why This Role
This is an opportunity to join a stable, well-established organisation at a point where customer insight and data-led strategy are becoming central to its future growth.  You will have the opportunity to shape the organisation’s customer intelligence capability, influence executive decision making and play a key role in building a modern CX function.

For a confidential discussion or further information, please contact Michelle Young on 0424 670680.

Six Degrees Executive acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of the lands on which we work and live. We are also committed to equal opportunity and strive to promote diversity, inclusion, belonging, flexibility, and accessibility in all that we do.

Should you require assistance in your interactions with us or through a recruitment process, please let us know so we can provide adjustments for you.

Financial Data Lead

We are seeking a highly analytical Financial Data Lead to support a strategic national project focused on analysing the cost and delivery of allied health therapy services. This role will lead the financial data collection, analysis and reporting required for a government pilot examining allied health therapy pricing and service models within the disability sector.

Working closely with senior leadership, finance, and operational teams, you will translate complex financial and operational data into clear insights that help inform national policy and future service delivery models.

Key responsibilities

Lead financial data extraction, validation and analysis from finance and operational systems
Develop cost models (e.g. cost per service hour, workforce cost drivers)
Prepare structured financial submissions and reporting templates
Work with the finance team, data analysts, IT, quality and compliance and operational teams to ensure data accuracy
Identify key cost drivers and produce clear analytical insights for leadership to enhance service delivery and financial performance
Assist with financial forecasting

About you

Strong experience in financial analysis, management accounting, or data analytics
Advanced Excel and financial modelling capability
Experience working with large datasets and multiple systems
Highly analytical with strong attention to detail
Ability to translate complex financial data into clear insights
Complex financial forecasting experience
Strong skills in using Microsoft Dynamics 365, Business Central, Microsoft Fabric and Power BI.

Experience in healthcare, government-funded programs, or professional services environments will be highly regarded.

This is a unique opportunity to contribute to a nationally significant project while working alongside a high-performing leadership team.

If you enjoy solving complex problems using data and financial insight, we would love to hear from you.

This role is located in our National head office in Gosford, 30 minutes north of Sydney. Work from home is available with an expectation of 2-3 days per week in Gosford. Free onsite parking is available.

About ORS:

ORS is one of Australia’s fastest-growing family-owned allied health, workplace rehabilitation & employment services providers. For over 30 years, we have been committed to offering evidence-based, client-centred services, and we’re proud of the incredible growth and success of our team.

With our inclusive culture, focus on employee well-being, and dedication to professional development, ORS is the ideal place for passionate professionals to thrive.

Does this sound like your ideal role?

Don't miss this opportunity! Apply now or reach out to Chris Galvin for a confidential discussion at 0477 010 235 or via email at Chris.Galvin@orsgroup.com.au. Interviews will commence the week of 23rd March.

The ORS Group is a multicultural, diverse, supportive and flexible employer, specifically encouraging applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Australians, people from culturally diverse backgrounds, working parents, LGBTQIA+ and people with disabilities.

Company: Management and Training Corporation Pty Ltd
Ad Text:
MTC is a global leader in corrections, immigration detention, education, and workforce development, with a proud mission to transform lives and strengthen communities. Our people are at the heart of everything we do - and we’re proud of the impact we make.   

About The Role  

As a National Risk Analyst at MTC, you will be responsible for conducting comprehensive national risk assessments to ensure the safety, security, and operational effectiveness across all MTC sites.   

This is a national role that offers a salary and level of responsibility commensurate with experience at the EL1-EL2 level.   

This role focuses on assessing people, property, and operational risks within the context of immigration detention and corrections. You will work closely with senior risk management teams and key stakeholders to inform high-level decision-making, drive national risk mitigation strategies, and ensure compliance with both internal and Department of Home Affairs standards.   

Key Responsibilities:  

Monitor risk assessment timeliness and trigger compliance across all centres 

Conduct quality assurance sampling of risk assessments 

Support structured assurance reporting for Senior Leadership and Departmental engagement 

Provide technical guidance on risk rating definitions, escalation thresholds and documentation standards 

Design and deliver risk assessment process training, scenario-based desktop exercises, onboarding and refresher training for site-based team members 

Support the drafting of national operational risk assessments and contribute to continuous improvement initiatives 

Collaborate with department leaders and external stakeholders to inform strategic risk management decisions.  

To be successful you’ll bring:  

Proven experience in a security or risk function within immigration detention, corrections or another complex operational environment 

Demonstrated understanding of contractual compliance requirements and performance monitoring frameworks 

Strong analytical capability, with experience interpreting operational data and producing structured reports 

Ability to design and deliver structured training or facilitate group learning sessions 

Solid understanding of national risk management principles and frameworks.  

Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to present insights to senior stakeholders.  

Proven ability to manage sensitive information with a high level of confidentiality.  

Formal qualifications in risk management, security, or a related field (desirable).   

Other Requirements  

Ability to obtain and maintain relevant police/security clearances  

Willingness to undergo drug and alcohol testing, and any required health assessments  

Must be double vaccinated against COVID-19  

Ready to Join Us?  

For more information about the role please email melissa.sloane@mtctrains.com.au

 Proudly promoting a culture of Safety, Respect and Dignity, TSS instils the BIONIC (Believe It Or Not I Care) philosophy in all our business dealings. BIONIC means promoting a caring culture for change based upon safety, respect and dignity.   

We celebrate diversity in our workplace and therefore welcome applications from all people who believe they have the skills and abilities to truly make a difference for our business.

The Opportunity

Our client is a prominent professional services firm with a strong presence across Australia. As the Commercial Analyst, you will be responsible for turning financial data into strategic insights. You will have visibility across all business divisions where you will play a key role in shaping key business decisions. You will bring your analytical skills, commercial awareness, and clear communication to drive real impact.

Joining this prestigious and fast paced business will help accelerate your career development and provide exposure to high-calibre professionals and complex business challenges.

The Challenge

Reporting directly to the Head of Finance, your role will include the following responsibilities but not be limited to: 

Deliver insightful financial and management reporting to support business performance and decision-making.
Analyse performance to identify trends, risks, and opportunities.
Drive production, utilisation, and revenue analysis to optimise operational and commercial outcomes.
Contribute to budgeting, forecasting, and monitoring of financial performance against targets.
Provide strategic insights through ad hoc analysis and commentary for leadership.
Enhance reporting processes, systems, and templates to improve accuracy, efficiency, and timeliness.
Support month-end and accounting activities, ensuring data integrity and reliability.
Partner with business leaders to translate financial data into actionable commercial decisions. 
The Expertise 

Please consider expressing interest in this position if you possess the following:

CA/CPA recent qualification
You will bring a minimum of 5 years experience, ideally in a professional services environment 
Strong analytical skills, experience using Power Bi will be highly regarded 
Attention to detail and ability to focus on quality work
Excellent communication with various stakeholders and strong interpersonal skills
Curious attitude and commitment to learning

The Next Step

Job Title: Lead the Future of Technology - Multiple Leadership Opportunities Available
Category:
Management (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - State (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
At SA Water, we are shaping the future of essential services for a sustainable and healthy South Australia, and technology is at the heart of that transformation. We are investing in bold ideas, modern systems, digital innovation, and enterprise-wide value creation.

To help drive this next horizon of change, we are excited to invite exceptional technology leaders to join us in one of three leadership roles within our Technology Strategy, Architecture & Value Realisation (SAVR) function. All three roles are ongoing and full-time.

We are seeking passionate professionals for the following roles:

Manager Technology Stream – Customer & Corporate

In this role, you will partner with senior leaders to understand organisational needs, shape technology demand, and translate strategy into clear, well-designed initiatives. Leading a team of Solution Architects, Business Analysts and specialist consultants, you will guide business case development, technology planning and architecture alignment to ensure investments deliver real value. If you thrive on collaboration, strategic thinking and building trusted partnerships, this opportunity offers meaningful impact across the organisation.

Manager Technology Stream – Industrial

Play a key role in shaping the technology that supports SA Water’s operational and industrial environments. You will collaborate with operational leaders to anticipate technology needs, build strong business cases, and guide the design of resilient, future-focused industrial technology solutions. Leading architects, analysts and specialist partners, you will ensure initiatives are grounded in sound architecture, safety, and real-world operational requirements. Perfect for a leader who enjoys solving complex operational challenges and enabling reliable services for the community.

Manager Technology Portfolio and Value Realisation

Lead the governance and optimisation of SA Water’s Technology Investment Portfolio. You’ll oversee capital planning, prioritisation, value realisation frameworks and portfolio-level insights that support informed decision-making across the organisation. Managing a specialist team, you will ensure investments align to strategy, regulatory commitments and funding constraints while tracking benefits, risks and performance. If you excel in portfolio leadership, financial planning and enterprise-wide collaboration, this is a high-impact role with visibility across SA Water’s strategic direction.

Further information can be found in the below Position Descriptions.

Manager Technology Stream - Customer and Corporate.

Manager Technology Stream - Industrial.

Manager Technology Portfolio & Value Realisation.

To apply, click 'Apply'. Please submit and complete the application form, indicate the role(s) you wish to be considered for, upload a cover letter and current resume. For enquiries, please contact Despina Livadiotis from the Talent Acquisition team Despina.livadiotis@sawater.com.au.

Applications close: 11:55pm, 29 March 2026

A great customer experience is best achieved by having a diverse and inclusive team that reflects the community we serve. Applications are encouraged from everyone, including people living with disability, job seekers of all ages, members of the LGBTIQA+ community, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, and people from culturally diverse backgrounds.

Job Title: Integration Analyst - Single Digital Patient Record
Category:
Developers/Programmers (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - State (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
Position: Integration Analyst (multiple opportunities)
Award: Health Manager Level 3
Employment Type: Temporary Full Time up to March 2029

Opportunity to work from our modern St Leonards/ Chatswood office
Attractive salary starting from $132,236 of up to $150,222 + 12% Super and annual leave loading
Allocated day off per month, salary packaging, discounted fitness passport and more!
Join the team enriching health in millions of ways every day through digital innovation

Applications Close: 11:59pm, 15 March 2026

About Us – Single Digital Patient Record (SDPR), NSW Health
Be part of the team leading the largest digital transformation in Australia’s public health history. NSW Health’s SDPR program is transforming the way patient care is delivered and experienced - creating a single, secure, holistic, and integrated view of patient information across the entire state. 

By connecting every corner of the NSW public health system, this groundbreaking program empowers clinicians and care teams with real-time access to unified patient data - enabling safer, more transparent, and quality care for every person, anywhere. 

Join us now and contribute to the millions of ways we're enriching health every day.
 

About the Opportunity

As an Integration Analyst of our Technology, Devices and Integration team within SDPRIA, you will oversee the planning, coordination and implementation of activities that support the integration of key applications and systems across NSW Health. 

This role offers the opportunity to contribute to large-scale state-wide programs through the analysis, development and support of system interfaces and integration solutions. You’ll work with messaging protocols, data models, and integration platforms to enable seamless information flow and support key digital health outcomes. 

In this role, you will:

Collaborate with technical and clinical stakeholders to deliver integration solutions that meet program needs
Support programs through the analysis, development and maintenance of application interfaces and technical workflows
Contribute to the resolution of complex incidents impacting integration architecture and ensure alignment with service-level expectations
Document business and technical requirements through integration data modelling and process mapping
Advise on integration-related risks, upgrades and changes to ensure system integrity and data security 

About You

We are looking for someone who:

Has experience working within a healthcare integration team supporting clinical systems and digital health programs
Has strong experience in business process analysis, data modelling and integration documentation
Brings a problem-solving mindset, with the ability to investigate and resolve complex technical incidents
Possesses excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills in a fast-paced, multidisciplinary environment
Holds relevant qualifications or equivalent experience in integration support within a large and complex organisation
Demonstrates a strong understanding of integration security practices and protocols 

Nice to have:

Experience developing interfaces using integration engines such as Rhapsody, InterSystems IRIS, Mirth Connect, MuleSoft, or similar platforms
Competence supporting production integrations, integration engines, or EMR integration modules
 

You will succeed in this role if you: 

Enjoy working in an environment where no two days are the same. 
Are motivated by tight deadlines and problem solving on the go. 
Can juggle multiple priorities while staying calm and outcome focused. 
Thrive when collaborating with others to deliver results in a fast-moving space.  

 

Need more information?

Position description
Check out our diversity and inclusion commitment
Learn more about our Single Digital Patient Record Program

Working at SDPRIA

Our people are at the heart of who we are. We support our people with great benefits so they can enrich health in millions of ways every day:

Professional growth and career development
Allocated day off per month
Salary packaging
Various health and wellbeing programs to support our team
 

How to apply

Share with us your cover letter and most up to date resume (up to 5 pages), highlighting your relevant skills and experience. 

We recognise that AI tools are increasingly being used to assist with resumes and applications. While we welcome their use, we’re eager to see your personality and get to know the person behind the application! 

Role-specific enquiries: If you have any questions about the role, please contact the hiring manager Moazzam Nawaz at Moazzam.Nawaz@health.nsw.gov.au 

General recruitment and adjustment enquiries: If you require assistance, have any questions about the recruitment or require adjustments to the recruitment/interview process, please contact Vaaruni Narayana at Vaaruni.narayanaiyengar@health.nsw.gov.au


Additional information 

This recruitment may be used to create a Talent Pool for similar future roles (ongoing or temporary) that may arise over the next 18 months.
To be eligible for this role you must have current Australian work rights (Australian citizen, permanent resident, New Zealand citizen with a current passport, or hold a valid visa with permission to work in Australia).
Successful candidates may be required to multiple virtual Epic training sessions and Epic orientation, all scheduled in U.S. time.
'Exempt' is defined as a fixed term contract with a length greater than 13 weeks.
This role may require evening, night, weekend and/or shift work, in accordance with operational needs and Award provisions.

Senior AI Engineer (Agentic AI / GenAI)
Location: Sydney, Australia (Hybrid)
Role type: Permanent, Full-Time
  
We're working with a well-funded AI company that solves real client problems. They're building agentic systems, deploying multi-agent frameworks and working with clients who have genuinely challenging problems to solve.
  
They are now looking for Senior AI Engineers to keep up with demand. Ideally, you should be an experienced Data Scientist (at least 3 years of experience) with a deep technical background in NLP or deep learning, including recent experience productionising GenAI and agentic AI projects.
  
The role
You’ll work in an agile environment with multi-disciplinary teams, including some of the most technical and experienced professionals in this field. Your key responsibilities will include (but not limited to):
  

Build and deploy advanced machine learning models that drive real-world impact for clients.
Develop and fine-tune NLP models using supervised learning (LSTM, CNN, Transformers) and topic modelling to extract meaningful insights and improve language understanding.
Build intelligent AI solutions leveraging LLMs and modern frameworks such as LangChain, LlamaIndex, RAG pipelines, and multi-agent systems to deliver scalable, real-world impact.
Provide guidance and mentorship to early-career data scientists within the team.
Establish and maintain strong MLOps practices to ensure performance, governance, and compliance.
Partner with cross-functional teams to design and deliver innovative data solutions.  
What they're looking for
The role requires a strong technical background in NLP or deep learning, coupled with agentic AI experience.

3+ years in a data science or AI engineering role
Recent, hands-on experience with GenAI and agentic AI
Solid Python, SQL, and NLP fundamentals
Production experience (not just POCs)
Strong communication skills
Relevant degree; Masters or PhD well regarded
Full working rights in Australia  
Why it's worth a look
If you want to spend the next few years doing genuinely interesting GenAI work rather than maintaining legacy models, it's a good fit.
  
Referral bonus: Know someone right for this? We pay $1,000 for successful referrals, DM us or email hello@algotalent.ai

About the university

We believe in the power of education to transform lives, strengthen communities and help people become the best version of themselves. The University of Notre Dame Australia is a modern, national Catholic university that serves more than 12,000 students across its campuses in Fremantle, Broome and Sydney.

With a community of over 1,000 academic and professional staff, Notre Dame is recognised for its personalised learning environment, strong graduate outcomes and holistic educational philosophy rooted in critical thinking, ethics and the Catholic intellectual tradition.

About the office

The Information Technology Office is at the forefront of the University’s success, driving cutting-edge technology solutions that empower Learning & Teaching, Research, and Administration across the institution.
 


About the role

The Head of Innovation and AI leads the design, development and delivery of the University’s AI, automation and digital innovation strategy. The role drives the responsible adoption of emerging technologies to enhance staff and student experiences, boost operational efficiency and accelerate digital maturity. This role is accountable for turning strategy into action by embedding AI‑enabled and automated solutions across core IT programs and enterprise processes to improve performance and outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

Develop and deliver the University’s AI and Innovation Strategy and Roadmap, while advising senior leadership on emerging technologies and their impact across teaching, research, and administration.

Drive a portfolio of AI and automation initiatives from concept to delivery, identifying opportunities that modernise workflows, reduce manual effort, and improve service quality.

Establish and maintain governance, ethical, risk, compliance, privacy, and data‑governance frameworks for AI, automation, and low‑code/no‑code platforms in collaboration with Architecture, Security, and Information Governance.

Build AI capability and culture by designing and delivering education, communication, and advocacy programs that uplift AI literacy and foster interdisciplinary collaboration among staff, students, and departments.

Oversee budgets, resource planning, and best‑practice lifecycle management for AI and automation, including model training, optimisation, monitoring, benefit measurement, adoption tracking, and KPI reporting to senior leadership.

Qualifications, skills & experience

Over 10 years of leadership experience, including more than 5 years in university environments, with a strong record of delivering large‑scale innovation initiatives across the higher education sector.

Postgraduate qualifications and/or equivalent advanced experience in IT, automation, or gamification, complemented by 5+ years of experience in Learning and Teaching or broader university functions.

Expertise in Artificial Intelligence, automation, and emerging technologies, with hands‑on experience designing and implementing AI solutions, particularly within the Microsoft ecosystem, to enhance academic and professional operations.

Strong innovation strategy and design thinking capability, with a proven ability to identify opportunities, design forward‑thinking solutions, and embed new technologies within enterprise-wide programs.

Advanced analytical, problem‑solving, and change‑leadership skills, adept at diagnosing complex challenges, influencing stakeholder buy‑in, and guiding organisational change.

Experienced educator and advocate, skilled in developing AI literacy programs, uplifting workforce capability, and ensuring governance, ethical compliance, and responsible technology adoption across the institution.

Benefits

Fast paced environment with opportunity for professional growth

Generous training and development opportunities

Friendly and collaborative working environment

How to apply

Please apply online with a resume and cover letter outlining your suitability for the role.

Applications closing date: 26 March 2026
